Fast cryptographic hash of Python objects, with a special case for fast
hashing of numpy arrays.
_ConsistentSetza Class used to ensure the hash of Sets is preserved
        whatever the order of its items.

NumpyHasherz7 Special case the hasher for when numpy is loaded.
    r
            Parameters
            ----------
            hash_name: string
                The hash algorithm to be used
            coerce_mmap: boolean
                Make no difference between np.memmap and np.ndarray
                objects.
        
	getbuffer)

NumpyHasher.__init__c
j	rH|
 Subclass the save method, to hash ndarray subclass, rather
            than pickling them. Off course, this is a total abuse of
            the Pickler class.
        r
HASHEDZ
_HASHED_DTYPEz

c_contiguous

uint8rU

strides
encoder%
obj_c_contiguous
klassr
NumpyHasher.saveN)
 Quick calculation of a hash to identify uniquely Python objects
        containing numpy arrays.
        Parameters
        -----------
        hash_name: 'md5' or 'sha1'
            Hashing algorithm used. sha1 is supposedly safer, but md5 is
            faster.
        coerce_mmap: boolean
            Make no difference between np.memmap and np.ndarray
    )
sha1zAValid options for 'hash_name' are {}. Got hash_name={!r} instead.rV
ValueError
formatr@
